text:
*[Bug 19020] New: R CMD check may load the old version of package with \Sexpr[stage=render]{self:::....}*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19020>

            Bug ID: 19020
           Summary: R CMD check may load the old version of package with
                    \Sexpr[stage=render]{self:::....}
           Product: R
           Version: R 4.5.x
          Hardware: All
                OS: All
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: minor
          Priority: P5
         Component: Low-level
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>

Created attachment 3628
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3628&action=edit>
Two ways to reproduce the problem

While experimenting with the fix for bug 18191, I found an interesting problem
that takes a few coincidences to trigger but is not impossible.

When I added a vignette engine to my package, the following NOTE appeared:

```
* checking package vignettes ... NOTE
Package has 'vignettes' subdirectory but apparently no vignettes. Perhaps the
'VignetteBuilder' information is missing from the DESCRIPTION file?
```

By the time `tools:::.check_packages` -> `run_vignettes` calls `pkgVignettes`
-> `loadVignetteBuilder`, the older version of the package, without the
vignette builder, is already loaded, and thus `length(vigns$docs)` ends up
being 0.

```
packageEvent(pkg, 'onLoad') |> setHook(\(...) sys.calls() |> format() |>
writeLines(stderr()))
tools:::.check_packages(paste0(pkg, '_2.0.tar.gz'))
```

...reveals that it's `.package_code_using_R_4.x_syntax` -> `Rd2ex` ->
`prepareRd`.

Moreover, if the "new" help page is incompatible with the "old" package (e.g.
because it calls a newly introduced function via `\Sexpr[stage=render]`), its
examples are not checked.

The following seems to fix both the vignette NOTE and the examples being
skipped:

```
Index: src/library/tools/R/utils.R
===================================================================
--- src/library/tools/R/utils.R (revision 89619)
+++ src/library/tools/R/utils.R (working copy)
@@ -2150,7 +2150,7 @@
                           ## Need to extract the code in the examples.
                           ## Rd2ex() does that and more, but provides no
                           ## output if there are no examples ...
-                          Rd2ex(u, p)
+                          Rd2ex(u, p, stages = NULL)
                           if(file.exists(p))
                               wrk(p, v)
                       }, error = function(e) NULL)
```

Two ways to reproduce the problem

While experimenting with the fix for bug 18191, I found an interesting problem

* checking package vignettes ... NOTE

, the older version of the package, without the

packageEvent(pkg, 'onLoad') |> setHook(\(...) sys.calls() |> format() |>
writeLines(stderr()))
tools:::.check_packages(paste0(pkg, '_2.0.tar.gz'))

.

Moreover, if the "new" help page is incompatible with the "old" package (e.g.
because it calls a newly introduced function via

), its
examples are not checked.

The following seems to fix both the vignette NOTE and the examples being
skipped:

Index: src/library/tools/R/utils.R
===================================================================
--- src/library/tools/R/utils.R (revision 89619)
+++ src/library/tools/R/utils.R (working copy)
@@ -2150,7 +2150,7 @@

                           ## Rd2ex() does that and more, but provides no

-                          Rd2ex(u, p)

text:
*[Bug 18928] Missing pieces in the C API for environments*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18928>

--- Comment #19 from Luke Tierney (<mailto:luke@stat.uiowa.edu|luke@stat.uiowa.edu>) ---
The dots patch is committed with minor adjustments in r89621 and r89622. I
believe this just leaves adding something like `R_envSymbols()` to get a vector
of the binding tags.

Many of the inspectors have to do an environment lookup on each call. If this
turns out to be a practical performance bottleneck we can look into caching
obtions or adding some form of iterator interface.

elements.text: [Bug 18928] Missing pieces in the C API for environments

of the binding tags.

obtions or adding some form of iterator interface.

text:
*[Bug 19020] R CMD check may load the old version of package with \Sexpr[stage=render]{self:::....}*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19020>

Sebastian Meyer (<mailto:seb.meyer@fau.de|seb.meyer@fau.de>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |seb.meyer@fau.de

--- Comment #1 from Sebastian Meyer (<mailto:seb.meyer@fau.de|seb.meyer@fau.de>) ---
> Created attachment 3628

(with a successfully anticipated PR number in the R script file names)

Thanks for the detailed derivation. In the recent r89412, I had already
disabled (build-stage) \Sexpr evaluation further above in that function because
it caused spurious warnings in other checks with packages using the new \bib*
macros. IIRC, the reasons were similar. So the change makes a lot of sense to
me.

I'll still work through your reproduction scripts before committing the change.
(BTW, thanks for using `package.skeleton`; one more case where the recent
improvements in R-devel -- returning the path and not requiring R objects --
make a lot of sense.)

text:
*[Bug 19019] interaction() should warn when coalescing levels*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19019>

Elin Waring (<mailto:elin.waring@gmail.com|elin.waring@gmail.com>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |elin.waring@gmail.com

--- Comment #1 from Elin Waring (<mailto:elin.waring@gmail.com|elin.waring@gmail.com>) ---
I wanted to note that 

```
ave(3:4, c("1.0", "1.5"), c("5.5", "5.0"))
```

returns 3 4.

As does
```
> ave(3:4, c("a", "b"), c("c", "d"))
[1] 3 4
```

Interestingly 
```
ave(3:4, as.factor(c(1.0, 1.5)), as.factor(c(5.5, 5.0)))
```   
returns 3.5 3.5   

```
> interaction(c("1.0", "1.5"), c("5.5", "5.0"))
[1] 1.0.5.5 1.5.5.0
Levels: 1.0.5.0 1.5.5.0 1.0.5.5 1.5.5.5

> interaction( as.factor(c(1.0, 1.5)), as.factor(c(5.5, 5.0)))
[1] 1.5.5 1.5.5
Levels: 1.5 1.5.5 1.5.5.5

> interaction( c(1.0, 1.5), c(5.5, 5.0))
[1] 1.5.5 1.5.5
Levels: 1.5 1.5.5 1.5.5.5

```

Would it solve the problem to cast the grouping variables to character?

elements.text: [Bug 19019] interaction() should warn when coalescing levels

) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |elin.waring@gmail.com

--- Comment #1 from Elin Waring (
Would it solve the problem to cast the grouping variables to character?
